global.title: epochs distribute revenue proportionally by weighted token supply
global.content:
# epochs distribute revenue proportionally by weighted token supply

## Objects Involved
- **Source**: Epoch — a time period with iPOWA supply, cPOWA supply, and a weight factor
- **Target**: DistributionRecord — a record of actual revenue distributed to an epoch's token contracts

## Relationship

Each epoch's share of total revenue is:
```
epochWeightedSupply = (iPOWA_supply + cPOWA_supply) × weight
epochShare = epochWeightedSupply / totalWeightedSupply × totalRevenue
```

Within an epoch, revenue splits further between iPOWA and cPOWA proportionally to their individual weighted supplies. A single revenue deposit creates one DistributionRecord per epoch.

The weight factor controls relative importance: weight=1.0 means full participation, weight=0.5 means half. This enables governance to phase epochs in/out gradually.

## Implications

- Adding a new epoch dilutes all existing epochs' revenue share (totalWeightedSupply increases)
- Changing an epoch's weight recalculates all epoch shares (zero-sum redistribution)
- Distribution records are immutable once created — they represent on-chain facts
- Dust (1-2 wei rounding error per epoch) is inherent to the proportional math and must be tracked explicitly

---

Relevant Notes:
- [[powa-revenue-distribution model captures epoch-based token revenue sharing]]
- [[masterchef accumulator pattern enables per-token revenue tracking]]

global.description: Each POWA epoch's revenue share is determined by its weighted supply (iPOWA + cPOWA supply multiplied by epoch weight) relative to total weighted supply
